q1: What is your overall experience with pickleball and other racket or paddle sports?
q1a: I have never played pickleball or a similar sport.
q1b: I have tried pickleball a few times or come from another racket sport but I'm still learning.
q1c: I play pickleball occasionally and know the basic rules.
q1d: I play pickleball regularly and feel comfortable in games.
q2: How well do you understand the kitchen (non-volley zone) and when you may volley?
q2a: I'm not sure where the kitchen is or when I can hit volleys.
q2b: I know the kitchen exists but often step in or volley when I shouldn't.
q2c: I usually respect the kitchen and only volley from behind the line.
q2d: I use kitchen rules confidently and choose when to enter or stay back on purpose.
q3: How comfortable are you with dinking and soft play at the net?
q3a: I avoid the net or hit hard shots instead of soft exchanges.
q3b: I try to dink but struggle to keep the ball low and in the kitchen.
q3c: I can sustain basic dink rallies and mix in occasional speed-ups.
q3d: I control dink height and placement and use soft game to set up winners.
q4: How confident are you with the underhand serve and basic serve rules?
q4a: I'm still learning the serve motion and often fault.
q4b: I can get slow serves in but struggle with depth, placement, or the two-bounce idea.
q4c: I have a reliable serve at moderate pace and usually let the return bounce.
q4d: I place serves deliberately and understand serving order and double-bounce rules in doubles.
q5: How would you describe your doubles positioning and match play?
q5a: I'm still learning scoring, side out, and where to stand with my partner.
q5b: I know basics but often leave gaps or stack incorrectly with my partner.
q5c: I cover my side, communicate, and understand common doubles positioning.
q5d: I adjust positioning with my partner, use stacking when needed, and play full games with a plan.
